summary: HR module verification PASSED end-to-end. /hr loads (no console 500s, employees موظف 1/موظف 2 visible after 'جميع الفروع'). Payroll report tab: colored cards including 'الدفعات المصروفة' (data-testid=card-total-paid) and 'المتبقي للموظفين' both render; employees table has 'مدفوع نقداً'/'المتبقي' columns with paid-{id}/remaining-{id} testids and a footer totals row. Payslips tab header shows 'مدفوع نقداً'/'المتبقي' and pending rows correctly show '-'. Disbursed 5,000 IQD via POST /api/payroll/payments to موظف 1 → report immediately reflects paid=5,000 IQD, net=16,667 IQD, remaining=11,667 IQD (net - paid), card-total-paid='5,000 IQD', card 'المتبقي للموظفين'='11,667 IQD', footer totals consistent. Attendance tab: created a check-in for موظف 1 on 2026-06-10; break-out-{attendanceId} and break-in-{attendanceId} cells render Arabic 'لا يوجد استراحة' as required for an employee with no break configured. Owner-treasury withdrawal untouched (linked_owner_withdrawal_id is created by the payment endpoint).

ui_bugs.issue: Badge shows 'الوسيط غير متصل' (offline) and console logs repeated ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for the realtime broker. REST APIs (employees/payroll/attendance/payments) all work fine, so functionality is unaffected, but the badge gives a misleading 'offline' impression to the user. Carry-over from previous iterations.
Branch combobox label renders as 'جميع الفروع1212' (count badge '12' concatenated without spacing next to the label, and twice). Cosmetic but ugly.

action_items: (LOW/cosmetic) Investigate the realtime-broker WebSocket connection in HR — badge stays 'الوسيط غير متصل' and ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED is spammed in the console.
(LOW/cosmetic) Fix the 'جميع الفروع1212' label by separating the count badge from the option label in the branch selector.
critical_code_review_comments: Attendance break-out/break-in cells use data-testid='break-out-{attendance_id}' and 'break-in-{attendance_id}' (the row's attendance record id), not '{employeeId}' as the review request suggested. Functional behaviour is correct; the discrepancy is only in the testid naming vs the request wording. Leaving as-is unless main agent prefers per-employee testid.
seed_data_creation: Created via API for testing: (1) attendance record id=cd3f23d6-... for موظف 1 on 2026-06-10 (check_in 09:00, check_out 17:00, no break); (2) salary payment 5,000 IQD on 2026-06-15 to موظف 1 via POST /api/payroll/payments (id=56e78486-..., linked_owner_withdrawal_id=62df4dae-...). These can be deleted via DELETE /api/payroll/payments/{id} and DELETE /api/attendance/{id} if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All HR features in this iteration are working end-to-end. Backend logic (net=16,667 with no deductions/bonuses/advances/overtime for موظف 1 with 500K basic on a fresh month) is consistent across /api/payroll/calculate, /api/payroll, and /api/reports/payroll-summary. The disbursement endpoint that the daily-payroll-tab UI calls is POST /api/payroll/payments (it auto-creates an owner_withdrawals entry). The realtime broker websocket being offline is a pre-existing environment quirk and does not impact REST functionality.
